summary.intent: Address PR review comments on GitHub PR #481 in the moltis repo, resolving all review conversations after making the requested code changes.
summary.outcome: All review comments were addressed with code fixes to callback_server.rs and server.rs, and all three review threads were resolved via GraphQL API.

repo: Error messages in gateway, TLS, and courier components should include the bind value for consistency — gateway was already doing this, TLS and courier needed updating.
IPv6 addresses like fe80::1 require a zone ID for actual binding; this distinction warrants a clarifying comment in code.
Test assertions for address parsing should cover negative cases (e.g., ':::8080', '::1:8080') and use semantic checks like is_ipv4()/is_ipv6() rather than trivial port assertions.

code.finding: Modified to include bind value in error messages and/or add IPv6 zone ID comment.
Modified to include bind value in error messages, aligning with gateway's existing pattern.

workflow: Review thread resolution requires GraphQL API (node IDs) rather than REST — a two-step process: fetch thread IDs, then resolve via mutation.
Work was done in a git worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/abalone-carrot, keeping the branch isolated.

friction: Resolving GitHub review conversations requires multiple API calls: first fetching unresolved threads, then getting node IDs, then issuing GraphQL mutations — no single REST endpoint handles this.
